title: Incident Response Plan Tabletop and Live Test Execution Logging

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=grc sourcetype="grc:ir_test" earliest=-400d
| where match(scenario,"(?i)payment|breach|PAN|CDE|PCI)")
| stats latest(executed_date) as last_run, latest(result) as outcome by test_id
| eval days_since=round((now()-strptime(last_run,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,1)
| where days_since>365 OR isnull(last_run)
| table test_id, last_run, days_since, outcome
| sort days_since
description: Indexes IR test outcomes and scenario coverage so annual (or more frequent) response exercises for payment breaches are tracked with timestamps, participants, and lessons learned for PCI readiness reviews.
value: Indexes IR test outcomes and scenario coverage so annual (or more frequent) response exercises for payment breaches are tracked with timestamps, participants, and lessons learned for PCI readiness reviews.

implementation: (1) Push test completion from GRC after each tabletop. (2) Adjust `days_since` threshold if policy requires semi-annual tests. (3) Link `test_id` to remediation tickets. (4) Board briefing attachment optional.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), `sourcetype="grc:ir_test"`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype="grc:ir_test"` ‚Äî `test_id`, `scenario`, `executed_date`, `result`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Push test completion from GRC after each tabletop. (2) Adjust `days_since` threshold if policy requires semi-annual tests. (3) Link `test_id` to remediation tickets. (4) Board briefing attachment optional.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype="grc:ir_test" earliest=-400d
| where match(scenario,"(?i)payment|breach|PAN|CDE|PCI)")
| stats latest(executed_date) as last_run, latest(result) as outcome by test_id
| eval days_since=round((now()-strptime(last_run,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,1)
| where days_since>365 OR isnull(last_run)
| table test_id, last_run, days_since, outcome
| sort days_since
```

Understanding this SPL

**Incident Response Plan Tabletop and Live Test Execution Logging (PCI DSS Req 12.10.1, 12.10.2)** ‚Äî Indexes IR test outcomes and scenario coverage so annual (or more frequent) response exercises for payment breaches are tracked with timestamps, participants, and lessons learned for PCI readiness reviews.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype="grc:ir_test"` ‚Äî `test_id`, `scenario`, `executed_date`, `result`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), `sourcetype="grc:ir_test"`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: grc:ir_test. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype="grc:ir_test", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where match(scenario,"(?i)payment|breach|PAN|CDE|PCI)")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by test_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **days_since** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where days_since>365 OR isnull(last_run)` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Incident Response Plan Tabletop and Live Test Execution Logging (PCI DSS Req 12.10.1, 12.10.2)**): table test_id, last_run, days_since, outcome
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (last_run), table (overdue tests), single value (tests past due).
visualization: Timeline (last_run), table (overdue tests), single value (tests past due).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
